title: Paris 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Paris 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Paris, France: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Eiffel Tower (summit access) and Louvre Museum. Prices from $2300 per person (estimate).
highlights: Sunset champagne cruise on the Seine
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Paris, France — Temperate oceanic climate with mild, damp winters (highs 7-8°C) and warm summers (highs 24-25°C); rain is possible year-round so pack a compact umbrella regardless of season. April to June and September to October offer mild weather, blooming parks and thinner crowds than peak summer. This itinerary blends Eiffel Tower (summit access), Louvre Museum, Notre-Dame Cathedral exterior with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Eiffel Tower (summit access), Louvre Museum, Notre-Dame Cathedral exterior
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Paris
itinerary.description: Land at Charles de Gaulle Airport (CDG) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Eiffel Tower (summit access) — Iconic iron tower with panoramic city views. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Paris: Louvre Museum & Palace of Versailles
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Louvre Museum — Home to the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo. In the afternoon, continue to Palace of Versailles — Opulent royal palace and gardens outside Paris. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Paris: Musée d'Orsay & Sainte-Chapelle
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Musée d'Orsay — Impressionist masterpieces in a former railway station. In the afternoon, continue to Sainte-Chapelle — Gothic chapel with stunning stained glass. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Palace of Versailles
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Palace of Versailles, a popular side trip from Paris. Return to Paris in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Palace of Versailles

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Marché des Enfants Rouges covered market — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Marché des Enfants Rouges covered market

itinerary.title: Paris: Arc de Triomphe rooftop & Catacombs of Paris
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Arc de Triomphe rooftop — Napoleonic monument with viewing platform. In the afternoon, continue to Catacombs of Paris — Underground ossuary tunnels. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Giverny (Monet's Garden)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Giverny (Monet's Garden), a popular side trip from Paris. Return to Paris in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Paris: Centre Pompidou & Seine River Cruise (Bateaux-Mouches)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Centre Pompidou — Modern and contemporary art museum. In the afternoon, continue to Seine River Cruise (Bateaux-Mouches) — Evening boat tour past illuminated landmarks. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include La Petite Ceinture abandoned railway — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Disneyland Paris
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Disneyland Paris, a popular side trip from Paris. Return to Paris in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Paris: Musée Rodin & Disneyland Paris
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Musée Rodin — Sculpture museum and garden. In the afternoon, continue to Disneyland Paris — Family theme park in Marne-la-Vallée. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Paris: Notre-Dame Cathedral exterior & Sacré-Cœur Basilica
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Notre-Dame Cathedral exterior — Gothic cathedral on Île de la Cité (interior reopened post-restoration). In the afternoon, continue to Sacré-Cœur Basilica — Hilltop basilica with free entry and city views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Musée de la Vie Romantique — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Montmartre streets or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CDG for your departure flight.

travelTips: Buy Louvre and Versailles tickets online in advance to skip long queue times.
Many museums are free on the first Sunday of the month from October to March.
Avoid dining right on the Champs-Élysées — walk a few streets over for better value and quality.
The Paris Museum Pass can save money if visiting 3+ major sites in a few days.
Metro line 1 and RER B (airport line) are known pickpocket hotspots — keep bags zipped and in front.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A compact umbrella for year-round rain
A smart-casual outfit for nicer restaurants
thingsToAvoid: The "gold ring" and "friendship bracelet" scams near Sacré-Cœur and the Eiffel Tower.
Unofficial ticket sellers outside major attractions — buy only from official websites.
Petition-signature scams (often a distraction for pickpocketing) near tourist landmarks.
Restaurants directly facing major monuments — usually overpriced and mediocre.
Walking distracted with a phone out near Châtelet-Les Halles or Barbès at night.
topRestaurants: Le Comptoir du Relais — French Bistro ($$$)
hiddenGems: Marché des Enfants Rouges covered market
familyActivities: Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie
Ménagerie du Jardin des Plantes zoo
adventureActivities: Bike tours through Bois de Vincennes
Hot air balloon at Parc André-Citroën
luxuryExperiences: Seine dinner cruise on a private yacht
Champagne tasting in Ritz Paris bar
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset champagne cruise on the Seine
Private evening Eiffel Tower dinner at Le Jules Verne
Romantic stroll through Montmartre at dusk
Couples spa at a Palace hotel
budgetExperiences: Free entry to Notre-Dame exterior and Sacré-Cœur
Wander Le Marais and Canal Saint-Martin
localEtiquette: Always greet shopkeepers and waitstaff with "Bonjour" before starting a conversation, even in English.
Keep your voice down on public transport and in restaurants — loud conversation is considered impolite.
Dress reasonably smart; athleisure and flip-flops mark tourists and can draw less attentive service.
Tipping is not mandatory since service is included (service compris), but rounding up or leaving small change for good service is appreciated.
Say "Bonjour" and "Merci, au revoir" when entering and leaving small shops — silence is read as rude.
localTransportGuide: The Métro is fast, cheap and covers the whole city — buy a Navigo Easy card and load individual tickets or day passes. RATP and Citymapper apps give real-time routing; taxis and Uber are widely available but can be slow in traffic.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to October offer mild weather, blooming parks and thinner crowds than peak summer.
weather: Temperate oceanic climate with mild, damp winters (highs 7-8°C) and warm summers (highs 24-25°C); rain is possible year-round so pack a compact umbrella regardless of season.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements well in advance; a Schengen visa issued by any member state covers France.
averageFlightTime: ~10.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Paris?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements well in advance; a Schengen visa issued by any member state covers France.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Paris?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October offer mild weather, blooming parks and thinner crowds than peak summer. Temperate oceanic climate with mild, damp winters (highs 7-8°C) and warm summers (highs 24-25°C); rain is possible year-round so pack a compact umbrella regardless of season.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Paris safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Paris is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Paris?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Paris is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset champagne cruise on the Seine experience.

reviews.author: Omar Patel
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Disneyland Paris and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Lukas Ibrahim
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Paris is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Sacré-Cœur Basilica. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Emma Rossi
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Paris was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Musée Rodin was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Noah Sharma
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Our trip to Paris was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Le Marais district was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Daniel Khan
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Montmartre streets and Canal Saint-Martin. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Sarah Andersen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Paris is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Pont Alexandre III. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Arjun Rossi
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Disneyland Paris in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Tom Kowalski

reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Pont Alexandre III and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
